item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Big, BIG +1. Do I detect a little madness in these mountains?

item.title: minor thing...
item.content:encoded: The article lists the beings as having latent psychic powers. Latent means not yet active, which I don't think is what you're going for. I enjoyed the rest of it quite a bit.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is a great SCP. I'd never read a Second Hytoth SCP before, so this was one Hell of a kickoff. It was a bizarre and horrifying adventure the whole way through, and I loved every last moment of it.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
One of the best SCPs I've had the pleasure of reading in a long while. This makes me want to take a crack at my own Sandboxes now, too.

Excellent work, OP!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
An absolutely excellent piece. You really exploit the loneliness and isolation of the Antarctic setting and create one of the best examples I've seen on-site of diary entries used for a more action-heavy, thriller-esque type of narrative. The juxaposition of the past and present elements is also really good; the ending especially transforms the curated, almost fictional-seeming diaries to something that feels much more real and direct.

Very easy +1. This should be read as an example of how to do exploratory/horror-from-time-based skips properly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Exceptional writing.
While some may complain about the length of the text and claim it's better off to be a tale, I quite enjoyed it.
I am a sucker for logs and journals, especially as detailed as here.
I got not a damn clue about "what" the skip in question is, but I like it very much.
Just enough mystery left to keep me wanting more.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Here's a long-ass article that's been sitting around in my sandboxes for far too long. I was originally planning on posting this last year, but during revisions I set about on a grandiose plan to make one of the longest SCPs on the wiki. Needless to say that didn't end up happening, and I decided to take the part of the draft I knew were solid, revise, and post. So here it is!

Thanks to DarkStuff for all their support and to MaliceAforethought for giving me last minute feedback before I posted! Thanks to Tanhony and Tufto for their prior crit. I can't remember every critter I had, so if you did crit just give me a shout and I'll list ya!

This is based on Church of the Second Hytoth lore and SCP-2651, but no prior knowledge of either should be necessary for reading.

+ Image sources
- Image sources
  • https://www.flickr.com/photos/internetarchivebookimages/20407013092/
  • https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Concordia_Station_at_Dome_C.jpg
  • https://www.jpl.nasa.gov/news/news.php?release=2013-077 (NASA image use policy)
  • https://www.flickr.com/photos/67193564@N03/6173953772/
  • https://www.flickr.com/photos/national_library_of_australia_commons/6173956626/in/gallery-mattiabattistin-72157628648579585/
  • https://www.flickr.com/photos/national_library_of_australia_commons/6173420793/in/photostream/
  • https://www.flickr.com/photos/national_library_of_australia_commons/6173948894/in/photostream/
